{"parts":[{"content":"Now I have a clear picture. Let me summarize and then scaffold the skill:

## Technical Reality Check

| Mermaid Diagram Type | Excalidraw Support |
|---|---|
| **Flowchart** | ✅ Native (rectangle, circle, diamond, arrows) |
| **Sequence** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |
| **Class** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |
| **State** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |
| **ER Diagram** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |
| **Mindmap** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |
| **Timeline** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |
| **Gantt** | ⚠️ Rendered as image |

**Key insight:** Only **flowcharts** get native Excalidraw hand-drawn rendering. Everything else becomes a rasterized image — which defeats the aesthetic purpose.

**Revised approach:** For native hand-drawn aesthetic across ALL diagram types, we should generate **raw Excalidraw JSON** directly when Mermaid flowcharts won't suffice. This is more complex but gives us full control.
